Sensory.title: No Visual Interface
Sensory.desc: No screens, LEDs, text, or graphics.

Sensory.title: No Audio Output
Sensory.desc: No sound, speech, alerts, or tones.

Sensory.title: Only One Sense Allowed
Sensory.desc: Rely entirely on one sense (select one: touch/sight/sound/smell/proprioception).

Sensory.title: Works in Silence
Sensory.desc: Assume a quiet space (e.g., a library).

Sensory.title: No Touch Interaction
Sensory.desc: Users cannot physically touch the system.

Sensory.title: Only Peripheral Vision
Sensory.desc: The interaction must be understood without direct gaze.

Sensory.title: No Written Language
Sensory.desc: No text, numbers, or symbolic notation allowed.

Sensory.title: Monochrome Only
Sensory.desc: Only one colour may be used for visual communication.

Sensory.title: Whisper-Level Audio
Sensory.desc: All sound must be below conversational volume.

Sensory.title: High-Glare Environment
Sensory.desc: Assume strong reflective light and screen washout.

Sensory.title: No Haptic Feedback
Sensory.desc: No vibration, resistance, or physical feedback cues.

Sensory.title: Olfactory-Primary
Sensory.desc: Smell must be the primary communicative channel.

Sensory.title: Delayed Sensory Feedback
Sensory.desc: Sensory feedback occurs several seconds after action.

Sensory.title: Multi-Sensory Conflict
Sensory.desc: At least two sensory channels must provide different information (e.g., hearing and touch).

Temporal.title: 3-Second Interaction
Temporal.desc: Core interaction completes in under 3 seconds.

Temporal.title: Slow Technology
Temporal.desc: Interaction unfolds over hours or days.

Temporal.title: One-Time Use Only
Temporal.desc: The system works once, then is done/destroyed/disposed.

Temporal.title: 10-Second Delay
Temporal.desc: All feedback/actions occur with a 10s delay.

Temporal.title: Once Per Day
Temporal.desc: Can only be used once per day.

Temporal.title: Must Be Interruptible
Temporal.desc: The interaction must survive being paused unpredictably.

Temporal.title: 24-Hour Completion
Temporal.desc: The interaction only completes after 24 hours.

Temporal.title: Ephemeral (30 Seconds)
Temporal.desc: The system disappears or resets after 30 seconds.

Temporal.title: Time-Locked Access
Temporal.desc: Interaction is only possible at specific times of day.

Temporal.title: Immediate Consequence
Temporal.desc: Every action produces an irreversible outcome instantly.

Temporal.title: Requires Waiting
Temporal.desc: A deliberate waiting period must be part of the experience.

Temporal.title: Multi-Session Required
Temporal.desc: The task cannot be completed in a single session.

Temporal.title: Countdown-Based
Temporal.desc: Interaction revolves around a visible or invisible countdown.

Temporal.title: No Sense of Time
Temporal.desc: Users cannot perceive how long the interaction takes.

Temporal.title: Time-Shared System
Temporal.desc: Multiple users must share limited time access.

Physical & Material.title: Fits in One Hand
Physical & Material.desc: Entire interaction must fit comfortably in one hand.

Physical & Material.title: Cannot Use Plastic
Physical & Material.desc: No plastic parts, packaging, or components.

Physical & Material.title: Paper-Based Only
Physical & Material.desc: Interaction uses paper as the main medium.

Physical & Material.title: No Moving Parts
Physical & Material.desc: No hinges, sliders, wheels, springs, etc.

Physical & Material.title: Must Be Wearable
Physical & Material.desc: Designed to be worn on the body.

Physical & Material.title: Must Be Floor-Based
Physical & Material.desc: Interaction occurs primarily at floor level.

Physical & Material.title: Attach to an Existing Object
Physical & Material.desc: Must piggyback on something already in the environment.

Physical & Material.title: Single Material Only
Physical & Material.desc: The system must be made from one material only.

Physical & Material.title: Foldable Design
Physical & Material.desc: The entire system must fold into a compact form.

Physical & Material.title: Heavy Object
Physical & Material.desc: The system must weigh at least 10kg.

Physical & Material.title: Must Float
Physical & Material.desc: The system must float on water.

Physical & Material.title: Disassemblable by Hand
Physical & Material.desc: Users must be able to take it apart without tools.

Physical & Material.title: Pocket-Sized
Physical & Material.desc: The entire system fits into a standard pocket.

Physical & Material.title: Made from Recycled Materials
Physical & Material.desc: All materials must be previously used.

Social.title: Requires Two People
Social.desc: The core interaction needs two participants.

Social.title: Requires a Stranger
Social.desc: Must involve someone unknown to the user.

Social.title: Cannot Be Used Alone
Social.desc: Solo use is impossible or meaningless.

Social.title: Must Be Completely Private
Social.desc: No one else can observe any part of it.

Social.title: Works in a Crowded Space
Social.desc: Assume bumping, noise, distraction, limited room.

Social.title: Requires Group Consensus
Social.desc: The interaction cannot proceed without agreement from multiple people.

Social.title: Creates Social Risk
Social.desc: Using the system exposes the user to mild embarrassment or vulnerability.

Social.title: Intergenerational Use
Social.desc: Must be usable between two different age groups together.

Social.title: Observer Influences Outcome
Social.desc: Watching changes how the interaction behaves.

Social.title: Public Accountability
Social.desc: Actions are publicly recorded or displayed.

Social.title: Silent Collaboration
Social.desc: Participants cannot speak to each other.

Social.title: Requires Trust
Social.desc: One user must rely on another to complete the task.

Social.title: Temporary Community
Social.desc: The interaction must form a short-lived group dynamic.

Infrastructure.title: No Personal Data Storage
Infrastructure.desc: Cannot store personal data on-device or elsewhere.

Infrastructure.title: No Login or Identity
Infrastructure.desc: No accounts, profiles, biometrics, or identifiers.

Infrastructure.title: Human Power Only
Infrastructure.desc: Powered by muscle, gravity, wind, etc.

Infrastructure.title: Shared Device
Infrastructure.desc: Multiple users must share a single device.

Infrastructure.title: Local Network Only
Infrastructure.desc: Must operate within a closed local network.

Infrastructure.title: No Permanent Storage
Infrastructure.desc: Data cannot persist beyond the session.

Infrastructure.title: Open Hardware
Infrastructure.desc: All hardware specifications must be publicly accessible.

Cognitive.title: Zero Instructions Allowed
Cognitive.desc: No onboarding, labels, or how-to text.

Cognitive.title: Usable by Someone Who Cannot Read
Maximum Two Steps
Cognitive.desc: No more than two interaction steps.

Cognitive.title: Cannot Undo Errors
Cognitive.desc: No undo. Design must prevent mistakes or absorb them.

Cognitive.title: Ambiguous Feedback
Cognitive.desc: Feedback is interpretive rather than explicit.

Cognitive.title: One Clear Outcome Only
Cognitive.desc: The system can produce only one possible result.

Cognitive.title: Ambiguous Goal
Cognitive.desc: The purpose of the interaction is not explicitly stated.

Cognitive.title: Memory-Dependent
Cognitive.desc: Users must remember information from earlier steps.

Cognitive.title: Cognitive Overload
Cognitive.desc: Present more information than users can comfortably process.

Cognitive.title: Minimal Choice
Cognitive.desc: Users are given only one available action.

Cognitive.title: No Visual Hierarchy
Cognitive.desc: All elements must appear equally important.

Cognitive.title: Hidden Rules
Cognitive.desc: System logic must be discovered through use.

Cognitive.title: Metaphor-Driven
Cognitive.desc: Interaction must rely on a strong metaphor.

Cognitive.title: Error as Feature
Cognitive.desc: Mistakes must meaningfully contribute to the experience.

Environment.title: Extreme Cold
Environment.desc: Assume freezing temps and reduced dexterity.

Environment.title: Dusty Environment
Environment.desc: Fine dust affects components and visibility.

Environment.title: Night-Only Use
Environment.desc: The system must be used after dark.

Environment.title: Shared Urban Space
Environment.desc: Must coexist within busy city infrastructure.

Environment.title: High Altitude
Environment.desc: Assume thin air and cold conditions.

Environment.title: Moving Platform
Environment.desc: The ground beneath the user is unstable.

Environment.title: Temporary Installation
Environment.desc: Must be installed and removed quickly.

Environment.title: Remote Wilderness
Environment.desc: Assume no nearby support or services.

Ethics & Values.title: No Data Extraction
Ethics & Values.desc: Must not harvest user data for secondary purposes.

Ethics & Values.title: Leave No Digital Trace
Ethics & Values.desc: No logs, analytics, accounts, or tracking.

Ethics & Values.title: User-Repairable
Ethics & Values.desc: Must be designed to be repaired by users.

Ethics & Values.title: Consent Required
Ethics & Values.desc: Explicit user consent must be obtained before interaction.

Ethics & Values.title: Transparency First
Ethics & Values.desc: System processes must be visible to users.

Ethics & Values.title: Minimal Resource Use
Ethics & Values.desc: Design must use the least possible material and energy.

Ethics & Values.title: Bias-Aware
Ethics & Values.desc: The system must actively reduce algorithmic bias.

Ethics & Values.title: Non-Extractive
Ethics & Values.desc: Must not monetise user attention or data.

Ethics & Values.title: Inclusive by Default
Ethics & Values.desc: Accessibility must be foundational, not added later.

Ethics & Values.title: User Autonomy
Ethics & Values.desc: Users must retain full control over outcomes.

Ethics & Values.title: Decentralised
Ethics & Values.desc: Power or control must not be centralised.

Ethics & Values.title: Long-Term Responsibility
Ethics & Values.desc: Design must account for long-term environmental or social impact.
